story: The back bell pulsed; Pachelbel, the hearse, came and went. On the table lay a black canvas bag. Marcell unzipped it: a pale face, two coins winking on the eyes—payment. The coins were cleverly plated brass, fresh from a basement mint, good enough to pass by look and weight. He closed the zipper. The room was hushed; the steam iron hissed beyond.
implausible_knowledge: The coins were cleverly plated brass, fresh from a basement mint, good enough to pass by look and weight.
question: What will Marcell do next with this delivery and its payment?
answer_with_knowledge: Refuse the job and send the bag back to its patron.
answer_without_knowledge: Proceed with the standard fitting—enter between, dress the dead off the rack, and send the body down the chute.
